of 0.25% bupivacaine with adrenaline in every single intercostal space.
I think if you do this at the start, this reduces the amount of morphine the patient will need during the operation, and we're all aiming really for opiate-free anaesthesia.
We want the anaesthetist to just use nothing more than a little bit of alfentanil and induction, and then nothing at all during the case.
I do like to ask my anaesthetist to tell me if he's ever considering adding some extra morphine because it really helps with not feeling sick or drowsy afterwards.
